clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.
The Service Provider shall develop the SaaS platform described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] for [PROJECT_NAME].
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [DELIVERY_DATE] at , subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.

The Service Provider shall implement industry-standard security practices in developing the platform, including [SECURITY_MEASURES], and shall notify the Client without undue delay upon becoming aware of a security incident affecting the platform.
Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Service Provider assigns to the Client all rights, title, and interest in the platform's source code.
Where the Client requests hosting, the Service Provider shall provision cloud hosting using [CLOUD_PROVIDER], with infrastructure costs billed separately at [HOSTING_FEE] per [BILLING_PERIOD].
Where the Client requests platform monitoring, the Service Provider shall monitor [MONITORING_SCOPE] and report incidents to the Client within [INCIDENT_NOTIFICATION_PERIOD_HOURS] hours of detection.
Where the Client requests ongoing DevOps support, the Service Provider shall provide it under terms agreed separately between the Parties.
Where the platform integrates [ANALYTICS_PROVIDER], the Client acknowledges that user data may be processed by that third-party provider under its own terms, separate from this Agreement.
Where the Client requests ongoing maintenance following launch, the Service Provider shall provide it for a fee agreed separately between the Parties.
Where the Client requests technical support beyond the Warranty Clause period, the Service Provider shall provide it at [SUPPORT_HOURLY_RATE] per hour.
Where the platform requires defined uptime commitments, the Service Provider shall target availability of at least [UPTIME_TARGET_PERCENT], excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
The platform shall be built using [TECHNOLOGY_STACK].
The platform's infrastructure architecture is documented in [INFRASTRUCTURE_ARCHITECTURE_DOCUMENT], approved by the Client before implementation.

Where the platform processes personal data of end users, the Service Provider shall implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect that data, and shall assist the Client in responding to end-user data rights requests to the extent the Service Provider controls the relevant systems.
The Service Provider shall notify the Client without undue delay upon becoming aware of a breach involving personal data processed by the platform.

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.
Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, or consequential loss arising from this Agreement.
Each Party's total liability arising from this Agreement shall not exceed [TOTAL_VALUE], except for loss arising from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — Security Clause requires specific measures via Dynamic Field rather than a vague "reasonable security" standard alone. Duplication: Security (general incident notification) vs. Data Protection (personal-data-specific breach notification) kept distinct since the latter carries heightened regulatory stakes justifying its own dedicated Clause. Loophole closed: Data Protection elevated to Required-when-triggered (Conditional Required) rather than a generic Optional Block, reflecting the near-universal regulatory seriousness identified in Step 1. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: Monitoring Clause requires an explicit incident notification timeframe, closing a gap where "monitoring" could otherwise mean silent, unreported observation. Complexity: Infrastructure and Technical Stack kept lightweight, referencing external architecture documentation rather than embedding technical detail in legal text. Enforceability: Security and Data Protection breach notifications both use "without undue delay," anchored by the surrounding obligation to act promptly upon becoming aware, consistent across both Clauses. Attack: A data breach occurs and the Provider delays notifying the Client for weeks — mitigated by explicit "without undue delay" notification obligations in both Security and Data Protection Clauses. Attack: Client assumes the platform meets some undefined "adequate" security standard, later disputed after an incident — mitigated by explicit, named security measures as a Dynamic Field rather than an unanchored qualifier. Attack: End user requests data deletion and Client has no path to fulfill it through the Provider — mitigated by Data Protection Clause's assistance obligation. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
